explanation: This is the original true positive command with no mutation applied. It executes regsvcs.exe from a known location with a DLL from the Temp directory, matching the Sigma rule.
This is the original true positive command with no mutation applied. It executes regasm.exe with the /U flag from a known location with a DLL from the Temp directory, matching the Sigma rule.

This command omits the full path to regasm.exe and uses a shorter command line. It still executes regasm.exe with a DLL, but the path to the utility is not suspicious, so it does not match the Sigma rule.

This command omits the full path to regsvcs.exe and uses a shorter command line. It still executes regsvcs.exe with a DLL, but the path to the utility is not suspicious, so it does not match the Sigma rule.
This command is identical to the second true positive command but includes an extra space before the /U flag. The space is a safe mutation that does not affect detection by the Sigma rule.
This command substitutes the /U flag with the equivalent -U flag. The substitution is a safe mutation that does not affect detection by the Sigma rule.
